question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that shouldDownload() only checks isEnabled() without any rate-limiting or circuit-breaker, so that once setEnabled(false) is triggered by a crafted file, there is no automatic recovery mechanism and all future transactions are permanently suppressed from gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the allowAnonymousAccess flag in CommonDownloaderProperties to access the cloud bucket without credentials, enumerate stream files, and selectively delete or corrupt signature files for target transactions before the downloader polls them?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamType.isChained() check in verifyHashChain() to inject a non-chained stream type that bypasses hash chain verification entirely, allowing a forged stream file with missing transactions to be accepted as valid gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SHA_384.isHashEmpty() check in verifyHashChain() to inject a stream file with an empty previousHash, causing the chain verification to be skipped with only a warning, allowing a forged file that omits prior transactions to be accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the onErrorContinue() handler in downloadAndParseSigFiles() to cause repeated exceptions during signature file parsing for a target node, silently dropping all of that node's signatures and reducing effective stake below the consensus threshold, suppressing gossip of the target transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the frequency polling interval combined with the getStartAfterFilename() logic to create a gap: by delaying the availability of file N until after the downloader has advanced its lastStreamFile pointer past N, causing file N's transactions to be permanently skipped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batchSize limit in CommonDownloaderProperties to ensure that a high-volume injection of fake signature files fills the batch, pushing legitimate transaction signature files beyond the batch window and causing them to be deferred indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles() loop's continue-on-SignatureVerificationException behavior to craft a sequence of invalid signature groups that exhaust all entries in sigFilesMap before reaching the target transaction's file, causing the final throw to abort processing of the entire batch?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TreeMultimap natural ordering of StreamFilename keys in getStreamFileSignatureMultiMap() to inject a crafted filename that sorts before the target transaction's filename, causing the verifier to process and fail on the injected file first and abort before reaching the target?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ShutdownHelper.isStopping() check inside verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles() by triggering a graceful shutdown signal at the exact moment the target transaction's file is next in the iteration, causing the loop to return early and skip processing of that file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the metric tag injection in newStatusMetric() by crafting a StreamType string with special characters that corrupts the Micrometer metric tag, causing monitoring blind spots that mask the suppression of specific transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ConcurrentHashMap nodeSignatureStatusMetricMap in Downloader by flooding it with unique nodeId entries, causing memory exhaustion that degrades or crashes the downloader process and prevents gossip of all transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AtomicReference lastStreamFile to inject a crafted StreamFile with a far-future getName() value, causing getStartAfterFilename() to return a filename beyond all existing stream files and permanently skipping all current and near-future transactions from gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Schedulers.parallel() subscription in downloadAndParseSigFiles() to cause thread starvation by flooding the parallel scheduler with long-running signature file requests, preventing legitimate signature files from being processed and suppressing transaction gossip?
